wiki_pages.markdown:
**Summary:** PREVENT ALL ALS is a prospective, observational cohort study enrolling individuals at risk for carrying inherited genetic variants known to be causative for ALS. Standard on-site clinical outcome measures include a neurological examination, handheld dynamometry (HHD and vital capacity (VC) and electromyography (EMG). Data also includes Edinburgh Cognitive and Behavioral ALS Screen (ECAS) and the CDR Dementia Staging Instrument PLUS National Alzheimer’s Coordinating Center (NACC) Behavior and Language Domains (Multidomain Impairment Rating Scale (MIR). Additionally, regular digital data collection of self-reported electronic Patient Reported Outcomes (ePROs) and speech measures were collected. Biospecimens include plasma, serum, RNA, whole blood for generation of induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) and DNA for whole genome sequencing, and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) via lumbar puncture (LP). The overarching goal of PREVENT ALL ALS is to prospectively collect, store and harmonize a large set of longitudinal data, which will be shared through the AMP ALS central portal, and biospecimens that will be linked to the clinical data for open science sharing with the research community. **Overall Design:** PREVENT ALL ALS is a single observational study with an optional sub-study for genetic testing results. PREVENT ALL ALS enrolled individuals from genetic ALS families who are either known carriers for ALS causative genes or who have not had prior genetic testing. Participants have regular in person and remote visits for collection of longitudinal biofluid collection and selected clinical measures.

[**DOI:** 10.1002/mus.28244](https://doi.org/10.1002/mus.28244) **Note:** ALL ALS Consortium dataset content on the ALS Knowledge Portal is dynamic and may change over time. While best efforts are made to ensure data quality, this data is provided 'as is,' and changes are periodically released.  Forms with active queries are not transferred from the ALL ALS Data Coordination Center to the ALS KP and may appear in future releases. Please review dataset DOI numbers and timestamps when requesting, downloading, or analyzing data.
